india_trends.title: Banks create buffers to cover war-related credit risks
india_trends.summary: Indian banks are strengthening their finances. They are creating special funds to handle risks from the West Asia crisis. This proactive step aims to protect against potential credit issues. Banks like Axis Bank, Union Bank of India, Indian Overseas Bank, and Indian Bank are setting aside significan

india_trends.title: Govt sets export duty on diesel at ₹23/litre, ATF at ₹33; petrol levy stays nil
india_trends.summary: The government has set export duties on diesel at ₹23 per litre and on aviation turbine fuel (ATF) at ₹33 per litre for the fortnight starting May 1, while keeping petrol exports duty-free.

india_trends.title: Mumbai property market hits new record with best-ever April performance
india_trends.summary: Mumbai's property market has achieved a record-breaking April. Over 14,160 property registrations were recorded, generating more than Rs 1,143 crore in stamp duty revenue. This performance highlights resilient end-user demand. Registrations rose 8.2% year-on-year. Revenue collections saw 3.3% growth

india_trends.title: Hindustan Laboratories receives SEBI observations for its proposed IPO
india_trends.summary: Hindustan Laboratories Limited has received SEBI observations for its proposed IPO of up to 1,41,00,000 equity shares. The funds will support working capital and corporate needs. The company primarily manufactures generic medicines for government institutions.

india_trends.title: Apple shares gain as investors await second quarter earnings
india_trends.summary: Wall Street analysts anticipate earnings per share of $1.95 on revenue of $109.7 billion. This would represent a 15% year-over-year increase from the $95.4 billion reported in the same period last year

india_trends.title: Stock market holiday on May 1: Are NSE, BSE, MCX open on Maharashtra Day? - The Times of India
india_trends.summary: Stock market holiday on May 1: Are NSE, BSE, MCX open on Maharashtra Day?    The Times of India

india_trends.title: Fino eyes asset-light, tech-led small finance bank
india_trends.summary: Fino Payments Bank is moving forward with its plan to become a small finance bank. The bank will focus on technology and secured lending, avoiding a traditional branch model. This transition is on track despite ongoing leadership reviews. Fino aims to offer home loans, gold loans, and business loans

india_trends.title: Gold loans surge five time in two years
india_trends.summary: Bank loans against jewellery rose nearly fivefold to ₹4.6 lakh crore at the end of March from ₹93,301 crore two years ago, registering a 123% year-on-year growth—the fastest across sectors, according to data from the Reserve Bank of India.

india_trends.title: Meta shares slump 10%, hit two-week low as AI spending concerns overshadow Q1 beat
india_trends.summary: Meta shares dropped 10.3% to $600, its largest decline in six months, amid fears that AI investments may not yield quick returns. The company reported Q1 sales of $56.3 billion but faced concerns about rising costs and declining user engagement.

india_trends.title: Mazagon Dock Q4 Results: Profit jumps 42% to Rs 464 crore; co declares Rs 4.62 dividend
india_trends.summary: Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders announced impressive March quarter results. Net profit surged by nearly 42 percent year-on-year. This growth was driven by increased execution in shipbuilding and submarine projects. Revenue from operations also saw a healthy 16 percent rise. The company's board recommended

india_trends.title: India plans ₹51,000 crore push to procure 62 ships amid West Asia crisis
india_trends.summary: The government wants to significantly scale up India’s shipping capacity, including container vessels, LPG carriers, crude oil tankers, and environment-friendly green tugs, as geopolitical tensions disrupt maritime trade routes.

india_trends.title: Sensex, Nifty end 7 per cent higher in April
india_trends.summary: Indices surge sharply across-the-board despite weak ₹, surging oil prices, FPI pull-out

india_trends.title: Laurus Labs Q4 Results: Cons PAT increased 19% to Rs 279 crore, revenue rises 5%
india_trends.summary: Laurus Labs reported a 19% year-on-year jump in net profit to Rs 279 crore for the March quarter, with revenue rising 5% to Rs 1,812 crore. Full-year net profit surged 148% to Rs 889 crore, driven by strong growth in its CDMO and Generics divisions. The company also announced an interim dividend of

india_trends.title: India sets course to procure 437 vessels by 2042
india_trends.summary: India is planning to acquire 437 vessels to increase its domestic fleet. Some vessels will be built in India, while specialized ones like VLGCs and VLCCs will involve imports initially. This strategy aims to develop local shipbuilding technology and skills. The procurement plan extends until 2041-42

india_trends.title: RBI accelerates gold repatriation as global trust wanes
india_trends.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is bringing home nearly 77% of its gold reserves by March 2026. This significant move accelerates the onshoring of bullion. The central bank has already repatriated a large portion of its gold. This action follows global events that raised concerns about storing sovereign a

HFCL posts profit of over ₹184 crore in Q4 FY26
india_trends.summary: HFCL said improvement in financial performance was driven by a favourable shift in revenue mix towards products, an increasing share of exports, and improved realisations in high fiber-count optical fiber cables

india_trends.title: FDI inflows set to top $90 billion in FY26, says DPIIT Secretary Bhatia
india_trends.summary: FDI inflows are set to exceed $90 billion in FY26, with April–February figures already at $88 billion, driven by reforms, free trade agreement progress and strong growth.

india_trends.title: IPO-bound JSW One acquires Pidilite-backed BuildNext for up to ₹100 crore
india_trends.summary: Though details of the deal such as share-swap ratio were not disclosed, the person cited earlier said that JSW One was valued at around ₹13,000 crore prior to the share swap execution. The transaction between JSW and Pidilite is expected to close by 10 August.

Oil supplies may hit a breaking point if the Iran war lasts into June
world_trends.summary: The buffer that the oil market has relied on following disruptions to exports in the Middle East could start to buckle as soon as early June, according to strategists at J.P. Morgan.
